title: Lingshed Monastery
tagline: Lingshed Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Lingshed Monastery or Lingshed Gompa is a Gelugpa Buddhist monastery in Ladakh, India.
overview.description: Lingshed Monastery or Lingshed Gompa is a Gelugpa Buddhist monastery in Ladakh, India. It is located near Lingshet village in the Leh district. It is 84 km north of Padum. It was founded in the 1440s by Changsems Sherabs Zangpo, disciple of Je Tsongkhapa, on a monastic site previously founded by the Translator Rinchen Zangpo.

topPlaces.name: Youl Chung
topPlaces.description: Youl Chung (also called Yulchung) is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.

items.note: Youl Chung (also called Yulchung) is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lingshed Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lingshed Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lingshed Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (71 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (202 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lingshed Monastery?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lingshed Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lingshed Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Lingshed Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lingshed Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Youl Chung.
